senses.definition: relating to both the heart and the lungs
examples: The doctor discussed cardiopulmonary health.
Many tests check cardiopulmonary function.

senses.definition: a medical procedure in which a needle is put into the heart, usually to diagnose or treat a problem
examples: Cardiopuncture may be used for diagnostic purposes.
The procedure is called cardiopuncture when a needle enters the heart.

senses.definition: relating to the heart and the pylorus (the part of the stomach near the small intestine)
examples: The report described cardiopyloric involvement.
This term links structures in the cardiopyloric area.

senses.definition: relating to the heart and the kidneys, especially when problems in one affect the other
examples: The study focused on cardiorenal problems.
They diagnosed a cardiorenal syndrome.

senses.definition: relating to the heart and breathing (respiration) working together
examples: Cardiorespiratory fitness depends on both the heart and lungs.
The team monitored cardiorespiratory function during exercise.

senses.definition: a surgical procedure that repairs a wound or injury of the heart
examples: Cardiorrhaphy was performed after the injury.
The surgeon recommended cardiorrhaphy for heart repair.

senses.definition: a medical term used to refer to inflammation or disease involving the heart (the exact use is rare and specialized)
examples: The historical text mentioned cardiorrheuma as a heart disorder.
Old medical writings sometimes use the term cardiorrheuma.

senses.definition: a rupture (breaking open) involving the heart
examples: Cardiorrhexis is described as a serious heart rupture.
Doctors investigated possible cardiorrhexis after trauma.

senses.definition: a shortened form used in medical or technical writing for terms connected with the heart (for example, “cardio-” in compounds)
examples: In the course notes, “cardios” appears as a shorthand.
The author used cardios in a technical context.

senses.definition: a developmental defect involving a split or abnormal formation of the heart (rare, specialized use)
examples: Cardioschisis is listed among rare congenital conditions.
The medical text describes cardioschisis as a heart malformation.

senses.definition: a condition where heart tissue becomes hardened or scarred
examples: Cardiosclerosis can affect how the heart pumps.
The doctor mentioned cardiosclerosis after test results.

senses.definition: a device used to view or study the heart (a specialized medical instrument)
examples: The cardioscope helps clinicians observe heart activity.
Older equipment included a cardioscope for examinations.

senses.definition: a spasm (sudden tightening) involving the heart or heart-related muscles (specialized use)
examples: Cardiospasm can cause distressing symptoms.
The report mentioned cardiospasm as a possible diagnosis.

senses.definition: a genus of plants (the scientific name Cardiospermum)
examples: Cardiospermum is a plant genus studied in botany.
The researcher described a new species in Cardiospermum.

senses.definition: a recording that shows heart activity and pulse (a specialized medical graph/record)
examples: The cardiologist reviewed the cardiosphygmogram.
A cardiosphygmogram records timing between heart beats and pulse.

senses.definition: a device used to produce a cardiosphygmogram (a specialized instrument for recording heart and pulse data)
examples: The cardiosphygmograph produced a pulse tracing.
Clinicians used a cardiosphygmograph for earlier studies.

senses.definition: an abnormal connection or fusion involving the heart region (rare, specialized use)
examples: The text used cardiosymphysis to describe an unusual heart fusion.
Cardiosymphysis is mentioned in a discussion of rare conditions.

senses.definition: treatments used to manage diseases of the heart
examples: Cardiotherapies include medications and procedures.
The clinic offers cardiotherapies for different conditions.

senses.definition: treatment for heart disease
examples: Cardiotherapy may include drugs and lifestyle changes.
They started cardiotherapy after diagnosis.

senses.definition: related to both the heart and the chest (thorax), especially surgery or medicine that involves them
examples: He works in cardiothoracic surgery.
They treated the problem with cardiothoracic care.

senses.definition: a surgical procedure in which the heart is opened or cut in order to repair something
examples: Cardiotomy is used to repair certain heart defects.
After the cardiotomy, the team monitored his recovery carefully.

senses.definition: making the heart work more strongly (said of some medicines)
examples: This drug is a cardiotonic.
Cardiotonic medicines can strengthen heart contractions.

senses.definition: a medicine that strengthens the heartbeat
examples: The doctor prescribed a cardiotonic.
Cardiotonic use requires careful monitoring.

senses.definition: medicines that strengthen the heart’s contractions (plural)
examples: Cardiotonics are sometimes used in heart failure.
The patient’s cardiotonics were adjusted after tests.

senses.definition: harmful to the heart
examples: Some chemotherapy drugs can be cardiotoxic.
The test showed a cardiotoxic effect.
antonyms: cardioprotective (if the context is about preventing heart damage)

senses.definition: a medical term (rare/technical) connected with the nutrition or nourishment of the heart (specialized meaning, not common in everyday English)
examples: The older medical text listed cardiotrophia as a concept related to heart nourishment.
Scholars use cardiotrophia in historical medical discussions.
